name: Nam Prik Makham Piag-Tamarind Dip
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Prik ki nu haeng daeng (dried red chilis)
recipeInstructions:
1. Use mature, brown tamarind paste. If fresh, peel and discard fibrous strands and seeds.
2. Store the paste in a glass preserving jar; avoid Tupperware to prevent staining.
3. Place 2 tablespoons of tamarind paste in a jug and add boiling water until it reaches 1 cup.
4. Let steep for 15 minutes, then mash thoroughly and steep again for another 15 minutes.
5. Strain the mixture through a muslin bag to extract juice (nam makham piag).
6. Pound dried shrimp, dry fry chilis, toast shallots and garlic until aromatic, peel and chop.
7. Fry shrimp paste briefly, combine all ingredients in a mortar or food processor until smooth.
8. Fold in tamarind paste mixture. Serve with fish dishes or crudites; keeps 2-3 weeks refrigerated.
